stem: A release enters the software release pipeline. The probability of passing code review is 77%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing unit testing is 88%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing integration testing is between 71% and 83% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing deployment is 89%. What is the overall probability that the release is deployed without incidents?

ground_truth.answer: P(deployed without incidents) ranges from 0.428 to 0.501 as the integration testing pass rate varies from 71% to 83%. Point estimate: 0.464.

solution_steps: The release must pass all 4 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3)×P(stage 4).
The uncertainty in the integration testing stage creates a 7.3 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the integration testing rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
